explanation: Classic stored XSS. If the payload is saved and reflected to other users, every visitor executes the script, making it persistent and high-impact.
Cookie exfiltration proof of concept. In stored XSS this would affect every user viewing the page, exposing all their session cookies.
Out-of-band cookie exfiltration. Creates an Image object whose src sends cookies to attacker server. The request is invisible to the user.
Fetch-based cookie theft. Modern alternative to Image-based exfiltration. Uses the Fetch API to send cookies to an external endpoint.
Redirect-based cookie theft. Redirects the victim to attacker server with cookies in the URL. More visible than image/fetch methods but very reliable.
Image onerror with fetch exfiltration. Combines the img error trigger with fetch-based data exfiltration. No script tags needed.
SVG onload with cookie exfiltration. The SVG element fires onload immediately and sends cookies to the attacker.
Variable-based Image exfiltration. Stores the Image object in a variable for potential reuse. Functionally equivalent to inline new Image().
Body onload exfiltration. Triggers when the page finishes loading. If stored XSS creates a new body tag, onload fires for every page visitor.
Autofocus input with exfiltration. The autofocus ensures immediate triggering without user interaction in a stored XSS context.
Details element cookie theft. The open attribute and ontoggle event fire immediately, sending cookies to the attacker server.
Marquee domain exfiltration. The deprecated marquee fires onstart immediately. Exfiltrates the domain name to confirm the attack surface.
Full-page overlay with mouseover. Creates an invisible div covering the entire page that triggers XSS on any mouse movement. Very effective for stored XSS.
Phishing redirect. Redirects victims to an attacker-controlled page that mimics the legitimate site. Effective for credential harvesting.
Base64-encoded payload execution. atob decodes the base64 string to alert(1), which eval executes. Hides the actual payload from simple string matching.
Deferred execution via setTimeout. The string argument is evaluated as code after 0ms delay. Some filters may not detect code inside setTimeout strings.
Function constructor execution. Creates a new function from string and immediately invokes it. Alternative to eval that may bypass eval-specific filters.
Document.write image injection. Writes an img tag into the DOM whose src includes cookies as URL parameters. The browser loads the image, sending the data.
XMLHttpRequest-based exfiltration. Uses the classic AJAX API to send cookies. May be blocked by CORS but the request still leaves the browser.
Meta refresh redirect. No JavaScript required. The browser redirects to the attacker URL after 0 seconds. Useful when script tags are filtered.
Repeating alert via setInterval. Fires alert every 5 seconds, making the stored XSS highly visible and disruptive to demonstrate impact.
Beacon API exfiltration. sendBeacon sends data asynchronously even during page unload. More reliable than fetch for exfiltration as it survives navigation.
POST-based exfiltration with context. Sends cookies and current URL in a JSON body, providing the attacker with both credentials and the page where the XSS was triggered.
localStorage exfiltration. Iterates all localStorage keys and sends each value to the attacker. Many apps store JWT tokens and user data in localStorage.
Base64-encoded cookie exfiltration via SVG. btoa encodes the cookies to handle special characters in the URL. SVG onload triggers immediately.
